overlook.name: Surface Overlook
overlook.description:
You emerge and the world hits you.

The sky hangs {pink:bruised orange-grey}, choked with dust. A ruined cityscape sprawls below — shattered towers, collapsed overpasses, streets swallowed by wild vegetation. The wind carries the smell of wet concrete and something organic, growing. {bright:Nothing moves.}

This was a city. Millions lived here. Now it's a graveyard.

Your {purple:implant} points west, steady and insistent.

examine.city: Skyscrapers reduced to broken fingers against the sky. Not bomb damage — neglect. 40 years without power or repair. Entropy won.
examine.sky: Dust and particulates from decades of unchecked fires, volcanic activity, industrial decay. The sun is a pale disc behind the haze.
examine.signal: Your {purple:implant} reads: {cyan:SIGNAL BEARING: 274° WEST — STRENGTH: 3/10}. Far, but reachable.
examine.vegetation: Scrub brush, hardy weeds, even trees punching through concrete. Nature is slowly digesting the city.

choices.text: Pick your way down into the ruins

crossroads.name: Crossroads
crossroads.description: An intersection of two cracked roads. Street signs bleached beyond reading. Rubble chokes most directions, but three paths remain passable. The asphalt radiates heat underfoot.

examine.signs: Sun-bleached and rust-eaten. But someone scratched an arrow into one with the word {amber:DUSTHAVEN} and a distance. Recent.
examine.smoke: Thin, controlled smoke to the east. A campfire, not a wildfire. Someone is living out here.
examine.rubble: A collapsed building blocks the western road. You can pick through, but it'll take time and attention.

choices.text: Follow the smoke to the east

rubble-field.name: Rubble Field
rubble-field.description:
A collapsed residential block. Concrete slabs and twisted rebar form a jagged landscape. {dim:Footprints} in the dust say scavengers work these ruins regularly.

Among the debris: a {cyan:crushed vehicle} with an accessible trunk, and a {cyan:partially intact room} exposed by the collapse. The sharp smell of corroded metal hangs in the air.

vehicle.text: A crushed sedan, half-buried. The trunk pops with effort. Inside: {amber:scrap metal} and {amber:copper wire} stripped from the electrical system.

room.text:
Someone's living room, open to the sky. A couch, a broken screen, children's toys scattered in dust. A faded family photo on the wall — they were happy once.

Beneath the rubble, something glows. A {purple:data cache} embedded in old infrastructure. A memory fragment, waiting.

examine.footprints: Multiple sets of tracks, some fresh. You're not alone out here.

choices.text: Pop open the crushed vehicle's trunk

encounter.introText: Something shifts in the rubble. A pale, eyeless creature drags itself from a crack — a {pink:Tunnel Crawler}, warped by decades of radiation. It hisses and lunges.

scavenger-camp.name: Scavenger Camp
scavenger-camp.description:
A small camp sheltered between two standing walls. A {dim:fire} crackles in a metal drum, its smoke funneled through a makeshift chimney. The warmth is startling after the tunnels.

{purple:Mara}, a wiry scavenger, looks up from sorting components as you approach. Not hostile — just surprised anyone else is alive out here.

examine.fire: Carefully maintained — the smoke chimney keeps the light signature low. {purple:Mara} doesn't want visitors she hasn't vetted.
examine.shelters: Two lean-tos. One holds a bedroll, the other is packed with organized salvage — circuits, wire, tools. A professional operation.
examine.camp: Small but disciplined. Everything has a place. {purple:Mara} has survived out here a long time.
examine.flashlight: A working {amber:flashlight} on a supply crate. Batteries are gold out here — this is a real find.

northeast-road.name: Northeast Road
northeast-road.description:
A wider road heading northeast. The rubble thins, giving way to cracked asphalt and dry scrubland. A hot wind pushes against your back.

A hand-painted sign nailed to a pole reads: {amber:DUSTHAVEN — TRADE WELCOME — NO WEAPONS DRAWN}.

The road stretches into haze. Long walk, but passable.

examine.sign: Hand-painted on scrap wood. Neat lettering — someone cares about first impressions. Below: {dim:Pop. ~200. Est. 2094}. Five years after the Collapse, someone chose to build instead of just survive.
examine.road: Cracked but usable. Vehicle tracks suggest traders run this route. You might hitch a ride if you're lucky.
examine.distance: About 12 kilometers. On legs stiff from 40 years of cryo, that's going to hurt.

choices.text: Head back to explore more ruins first
